title: Note Receivable — Accept and Honor
event: On November 1, Reyes Co. accepts a $10,000, 8%, 90-day note from a customer to settle a past-due account. The note is honored at maturity. Prepare the entries (use a 360-day year).

lines.label: formal promise
lines.why: The open account upgrades to a formal, interest-bearing note.
lines.trap: Face value only — interest hasn't been earned yet on day one.

lines.label: old claim replaced
lines.why: The past-due account is settled by the note.
lines.trap: Not Sales Revenue — nothing new was sold; an old claim changed form.

lines.label: principal + interest
lines.why: Maturity value collected: face plus 90 days of interest.
lines.trap: Maturity value = principal + interest — not face alone.

lines.label: note settled
lines.why: The note comes off the books at face.
lines.trap: Credit the note at FACE — the interest has its own line.

lines.label: 90 days earned
lines.trap: The 8% is ANNUAL — the 90/360 fraction is where points die.

memorize.body: Interest = Principal × annual rate × time/360 (banker's year in this text).
If the note straddled year-end, the interest would split via an accrual — earned by calendar, not by collection.

distractors.feedback: That's a full YEAR of interest. This note ran 90 days: 800 × 90/360 = 200.
You used 90/365 days against a different base — this text uses the 360-day banker's year: 200.

build.scaffold: Accept at face against AR. Honor: cash for maturity value, split face vs interest.
